mission.title: Install and Configure NATS
mission.description: NATS is a high-performance messaging system designed for cloud-native applications. It is ideal for microservices architectures, IoT, and edge computing, providing low-latency communication. Key trade-offs include simplicity and performance versus advanced features found in more complex messaging systems.

steps.title: Add NATS Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official NATS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add nats https://nats-io.github.io/nats-helm

```

> This is the official repository for NATS Helm charts.

steps.title: Install NATS Server
steps.description:
This command installs the NATS server using Helm with a specified version and namespace.

```bash

helm install nats nats/nats --version 2.11.0 --namespace nats --create-namespace

```

> Replace '2.11.0' with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create a NATS Configuration Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource for NATS configuration to define specific settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a NATS cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify NATS Server Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the NATS pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> You should see 3 NATS pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NATS Server
uninstall.description:
This command removes the NATS server and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in NATS.

```bash

helm uninstall nats --namespace nats

kubectl delete namespace nats

```

> This will remove the NATS release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade NATS Server
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your NATS configuration and data. This step will upgrade the NATS server to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade nats nats/nats --version 2.11.0 --namespace nats

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Drains the NATS pods before upgrade.
> Replace '2.11.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that the NATS pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n nats -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: NATS pods are in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the NATS custom resource.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the NATS pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n nats' to identify configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to NATS server
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to network policies or service misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the NATS service is correctly configured and accessible from your application pods.

troubleshooting.title: NATS server is not scaling
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The NATS custom resource may not have the correct size specified.

**Fix:**
Update the NATS custom resource to increase the size and apply the changes.

troubleshooting.title: NATS server is not responding
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to resource constraints on the pods.

**Fix:**
Check resource limits and requests in the NATS deployment and adjust as necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for JetStream improvements and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10, review the JetStream migration guide for necessary changes.

resolution.summary: You should see the NATS pods running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n nats' showing 'Running' status for all pods.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
